title: To provide additional funding for scholarships for students at 1890 institutions, and for other purposes.
plain_summary: This bill gives more money to help students pay for college at historically Black universities that are part of the land-grant system. These schools were created in 1890 and focus on teaching about food and farming. Starting in 2025, the government will give fifteen million dollars every year for scholarships. The money can help students pay for both undergraduate and graduate degrees in food and agricultural sciences. The bill also makes sure this scholarship program will continue forever without needing approval again.
last_action_text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Conservation, Research, and Biotechnology.

sections.title: Section 1 Additional Funding for Scholarships for Students at 1890 Institutions
sections.text: This section changes the rules for scholarships given to students at 1890 institutions. These are historically Black colleges and universities that were created in 1890. The changes make it clear that scholarships can be given to students working toward both bachelor degrees and graduate degrees like master's or doctoral degrees. The section also creates new mandatory funding of fifteen million dollars starting in fiscal year 2025 and every year after that. This money comes from the Commodity Credit Corporation and will stay available until it is spent. The section removes time limits on discretionary funding that Congress can choose to give, making it available from fiscal year 2020 onward instead of ending in 2023. Finally, it changes the scholarship terms so students can receive scholarships for each academic year as long as funding is available, instead of being limited to just four years.
